items.title: OpenAI confirms Sora app shutting down April 26 — API sunset in September
items.why_it_matters: OpenAI killing its flagship video product signals a strategic pivot toward coding tools and enterprise — compute is finite.
items.brief: OpenAI is discontinuing Sora: web/app closes April 26, API closes September 24. The product was burning ~$1M/day with <500K users.
items.details: OpenAI confirmed the two-stage shutdown of Sora. The web and app experiences close April 26, 2026, and the API follows September 24. Reports indicate Sora peaked at ~1M users before collapsing to under 500K while costing roughly $1M/day in compute. OpenAI is redirecting those resources toward coding tools and a unified ChatGPT 'super app.' If you built on Sora's API, start migrating to Google Veo or alternatives now.

items.title: Claude Code subscribers will need to pay extra for third-party tool usage (OpenClaw)
items.why_it_matters: Changes the economics for developers using Claude Code with third-party integrations — budget impact for power users.
items.brief: Starting April 4, Claude subscription limits no longer cover third-party harnesses like OpenClaw — additional costs will apply.
items.details: TechCrunch reports Anthropic is separating Claude Code subscription usage from third-party tool consumption. Subscribers who use tools like OpenClaw will face additional charges beyond their base subscription. This pricing change reflects the compute cost of agent tool-calling at scale and may push some developers toward self-hosted alternatives or prompt them to optimize tool call frequency.

items.title: Google launches Veo 3.1 Lite — video generation at half the cost of Veo 3.1 Fast
items.why_it_matters: Dramatically lowers the cost barrier for building AI video into products, timed perfectly as OpenAI exits the space with Sora.
items.brief: Google released Veo 3.1 Lite at $0.05/sec for 720p — less than half the price of Veo 3.1 Fast — as OpenAI discontinues Sora.
items.details: Google introduced Veo 3.1 Lite as its most cost-effective video generation model, priced at $0.05 per second for 720p video. This is less than half the cost of Veo 3.1 Fast ($0.10/sec). The timing is strategic: it launches just as OpenAI confirms Sora's shutdown, positioning Google as the default for AI video generation. If you were evaluating Sora, Veo 3.1 Lite is the obvious migration target.

items.title: Q1 2026 venture funding shatters records — $300B globally, 87% AI-related
items.why_it_matters: AI continues to dominate venture investment at historic levels — signals sustained infrastructure buildout ahead.
items.brief: Investors poured $300B into 6,000 startups in Q1 2026, up 150%+ YoY. More than 87% went to AI-related companies.
items.details: Crunchbase reports Q1 2026 set an all-time global venture funding record at $300 billion across 6,000 startups. U.S. and Canadian companies alone secured $252.6B. Over 87% of investment went to AI-related categories. Startup M&A was also strong at $56.6B in exits. This level of investment indicates the AI infrastructure buildout is accelerating, not plateauing.
